mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces corporate borrowing costs and lowers equity discount rates, mechanically driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of declining rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where unemployment rises >0.5pp, or signal loses predictive power with p > 0.05 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand recovery, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant with p > 0.10 in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, a decline in the industrial production index reflects constrained supply-side capacity and reduced corporate output, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during tightening cycles, or 5-day forward returns show no significant difference between declining and stable industrial production periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >65% of high-inflation months, or market pricing of rate hikes becomes decoupled from CPI prints with Rank IC < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm robust labor market expansion, supporting corporate earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns in >55% of observations, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects expanding economic activity and corporate revenue fundamentals, which mechanically supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >60% of positive GDP growth quarters, or the signal exhibits zero predictive accuracy with Rank IC near 0 across rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production accelerates above 0.3% monthly and housing starts exceed 1.5 million units, it signals synchronized supply and demand recovery, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined threshold condition yields negative forward returns in >50% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance after adjusting for market volatility regimes.
